content.full_text:
April 19, 1991

Mark Morodomi, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Morodomi:

This letter requests written confirmation of the advice you gave me over the telephone on Tuesday, April 2, 1991, pursuant to Government Code section 83114 and 2 California Code of Regulations section 18329.

I had inquired about the campaign reporting provisions of the Political Reform Act. Specifically, our firm represents companies which are members of "breakfast clubs" organized by local candidates. A company "joins" a candidate's breakfast club by giving the candidate's campaign committee a "membership fee" at the beginning of the year. Both the company and the candidate report this membership fee as a contribution. Then, the candidate organizes breakfast meetings throughout the year, where the candidate discusses local government issues with the club's members.

I asked whether members must disclose the cost of these breakfasts as a contribution to the candidate. I offered two scenarios: (1) Members pay the restaurant directly for the cost of their meal, and (2) Members write a check to the candidate's campaign committee for the cost of their meal, and the committee forwards the checks to the restaurant. In both of these scenarios, the amount given to the restaurant or the committee covers only the cost of the member's meal; no portion of the payment from the members goes to the committee.

Mark Morodomi, Esq.
April 19, 1991
Page 2

You indicated that, in either situation described above, the cost of the meal would not be considered a contribution under section 82015. In the first scenario, the payments are not reported by either the members or the committee. In the second situation, however, where the committee receives checks made out to the committee and then writes its own check to the restaurant, the committee would have to disclose the payments as "Miscellaneous Increases to Cash" and "Expenditures" on its campaign reports. The members would still have no reporting obligation.

If this letter does not accurately describe your advice, please call me immediately. We look forward to receiving your written confirmation.

Sincerely,

James R. Sutton

JRS/cn
